title: Battery & Charging
description: Battery life expectations, charging instructions, and low battery alerts.

sections.body: Different Plantgotchi sensors have different power sources. Here's what to expect and how to keep them running.

sections.heading: Battery Life by Sensor
sections.body:
SensorPower SourceExpected Life
BLE ChildCR2032 coin cell~2 years
SoloUSB-C rechargeableVaries by usage
PlantCamInternal battery4–6 months
PlantCam ProUSB-C (always on)N/A — wall powered

sections.heading: Charging the Solo Sensor
sections.body: Plug any USB-C cable into the bottom of the Solo sensor. A full charge takes about 1–2 hours. The LED turns solid green when fully charged. You can leave the sensor in soil while charging — just run the cable along the pot edge.

sections.heading: Replacing the CR2032 (BLE Child)
sections.body:
1. Gently twist the sensor housing to open the battery compartment.
  2. Remove the old CR2032 coin cell.
  3. Insert a new CR2032 with the positive (+) side facing up.
  4. Close the housing. The sensor will begin advertising over BLE within seconds.

sections.heading: Low Battery Alerts
sections.body:
Plantgotchi monitors battery levels automatically. The rule engine triggers alerts at two thresholds:

  • Below 15% — Warning severity: "Sensor battery low — charge soon"
  • Below 5% — Critical severity: urgent notification to charge immediately

These alerts appear as recommendations in your plant's detail view and in push notifications (if enabled on mobile).

sections.heading: Maximizing Battery Life
sections.body:
• Increase the BLE notification interval (60s instead of 30s) via sensor config.
  • Keep sensors within BLE range — connection retries drain battery faster.
  • For WiFi sensors, a stable WiFi connection uses less power than frequent reconnects.
